old_claim.note: The previous constellation graph omitted the underlying artificial-satellite platform while attaching later application classes instead.

new_claim.note: A satellite constellation is constituted by multiple artificial satellites operated together, so satellite technology is a required platform rather than an optional enabler.

source_shape.source_locator: Vela history: describes twelve artificial satellites launched as six coordinated pairs, beginning with the first pair on October 17, 1963.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The official history documents the scoped constellation as multiple orbital satellites deliberately deployed and operated in coordinated pairs.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Removing artificial-satellite capability would remove every physical member of the constellation; communications and CubeSat implementations are not substitutes for that platform requirement.

ontology_before: Satellite constellations lacked their constitutive satellite-platform dependency and instead pointed to later implementation categories.
ontology_after: Satellite technology is the required physical platform for the coordinated multi-satellite system class.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: satellite_technology remains required for satellite_constellations.
would_reject_if: The node is broadened to coordinated non-satellite vehicles or sensors rather than orbital spacecraft.
The Vela pair is shown not to consist of artificial satellites operated together.
short_reason: A satellite constellation necessarily consists of multiple artificial satellites.
